task: I need to send a short update to a client about a paperwork delay, and I want to make sure it covers the important points without missing anything I should mention. Can you help me put together a draft I can send today?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a short draft you can send today:

Hi [Client Name],

Quick update: there’s a delay with the paperwork due to [brief reason]. I’m working to get it resolved and expect to have it to you by [new date/time].

Nothing should change on your end for now, but I’ll let you know right away if anything does.

Best,  
[Your Name]

If you want, I can also make this a little more polished or more casual.

user_simulator.text: That’s the basic shape, but it’s a little too generic for my use. I’d want it to say whether the delay is from my side or theirs, and whether I’ve already got everything I need or I’m still waiting on one piece. Also maybe mention if the deadline shifts because of it. Short is fine — just don’t leave the client guessing.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter version that covers those points:

Hi [Client Name],

Quick update: the paperwork is delayed on my side because I’m still waiting on [one item / one piece of information] from [my team / you / the other party]. I already have everything else I need, and the timeline has shifted to [new date/time].

Nothing else should change for you right now, but I’ll keep you posted as soon as I have it.

Best,  
[Your Name]

If you want, I can also give you:
- a version that sounds a little more polished
- a version that’s more direct/plain
- one with “your side” vs “my side” already filled in

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. It’s short, and it says the key thing without making them chase it down.
